mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate signal a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate above its recent low indicates a weakening labor market and potential recession, which typically precedes a decline in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 3.5%, near historical lows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling a slowdown in economic activity that negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, persistent high CPI readings create uncertainty about future monetary policy and erode real earnings, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns as investors price in continued tightening and economic headwinds.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL 12-month % change > 5%, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and robust supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings and overall economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in real GDP growth after a period of contraction signals an improving economic outlook, boosting investor confidence and corporate profit expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly % change (annualized) turns positive after two consecutive negative quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in non-farm payrolls combined with an aggressively rising federal funds rate suggests an overheating economy where the central bank is actively trying to cool demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to higher borrowing costs and recession fears.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 200k AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is strong, FEDFUNDS is rising.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with aggressive monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: An exceptionally high federal funds rate, indicating severe monetary tightening, significantly increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to strong negative S&P 500 forward returns in the immediate term as markets react to restrictive policy.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in this regime.
